skill.name: libreoffice-impress
skill.description: Create, edit, inspect, export, and snapshot LibreOffice Impress presentations through Python UNO automation.
skill.summary: Automate LibreOffice Impress decks with structured slide editing, patch workflows, export, and snapshots.
skill.author: dfk1352

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found no prompt injection, network exfiltration, credential harvesting, or malicious persistence. The confirmed risks are legitimate for this office automation skill: it launches local LibreOffice processes, reads an environment path for UNO support, and writes temporary and output files. The weak-cryptography and Ruby backtick detections appear to be false positives from documentation, UNO names, string constants, and Python code examples.

medium_findings.title: Controlled Local LibreOffice Process Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill starts local LibreOffice processes for UNO sessions and PNG conversion. This is required for Impress automation and uses argument lists rather than shell strings, but it still executes host software and should run only on trusted presentation inputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are explicit and semantically required for LibreOffice automation. Risk is reduced because arguments are passed as lists and no shell invocation is used.

medium_findings.title: Environment-Controlled UNO Module Path
medium_findings.description: The skill reads LIBREOFFICE_PROGRAM_PATH and can add that directory to sys.path while resolving the UNO module. This supports nonstandard LibreOffice installs, but a hostile environment value could influence which local Python module is imported.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable is directly appended to candidate import paths after directory checks. This is a legitimate configuration pattern, but it depends on a trusted runtime environment.

low_findings.title: Temporary File and Profile Handling
low_findings.description: The skill creates temporary files and a temporary LibreOffice user profile during snapshot and UNO operations. The reviewed code uses Python tempfile helpers and removes the temporary profile during cleanup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is clear and bounded to temporary processing. Cleanup is visible for the LibreOffice profile, and TemporaryDirectory handles snapshot cleanup.

low_findings.title: LibreOffice Path Discovery
low_findings.description: The skill searches PATH and common install locations to find LibreOffice. This is local capability discovery for the declared feature, not broad system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The function only checks for expected soffice or libreoffice executable locations. No network calls, data collection, or persistence behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Static High-Severity Detections Dismissed as False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography and Ruby backtick detections do not correspond to cryptographic code or Ruby shell execution in the reviewed context. They match documentation text, UNO API strings, method names, and Python examples for the intended presentation workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary API documentation and UNO operations at these locations. No hashing, encryption, Ruby execution, or shell metacharacter execution was found in the cited context.

content.value_statement: Manual presentation updates are slow and error prone at scale. This skill uses LibreOffice UNO automation to create, edit, export, and verify Impress decks.
actual_capabilities: Create new Impress presentations and count slides.
Open session-based editing workflows for existing ODP decks.
Add, delete, move, duplicate, and inspect slides with structured targets.
Insert and update text, lists, shapes, images, tables, charts, media, and speaker notes.
Apply ordered patch operations in atomic or best-effort mode.
Export decks to PDF or PPTX and capture slide PNG snapshots.
limitations: Requires LibreOffice with Python UNO support on the host machine.
All UNO-facing presentation and asset paths must be absolute.
Snapshot rendering can differ slightly from requested pixel dimensions.
Shape names can change after native LibreOffice duplication.

use_cases.title: Automated Business Reports
use_cases.description: Generate recurring ODP reports with updated text, tables, charts, and exported PDFs.

use_cases.title: Bulk Deck Maintenance
use_cases.description: Apply consistent edits across slide decks, such as updated wording, speaker notes, layouts, and media.

use_cases.title: Presentation QA Workflows
use_cases.description: Capture slide snapshots and inspect slide inventory after automated changes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new LibreOffice Impress presentation at /absolute/path/status.odp with a title slide and two content slides.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for starting a new ODP file.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Open /absolute/path/report.odp and replace the title text on slide 0 with Monthly Operations Review.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple edit to a known slide target.

prompt_templates.title: Patch Several Slide Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply an atomic patch to /absolute/path/deck.odp that updates slide 2 body text, adds speaker notes, and inserts a table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for repeatable deck edits.

prompt_templates.title: Export and Verify Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Export /absolute/path/final.odp to /absolute/path/final.pdf and capture a PNG snapshot of slide 3 for layout review.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for delivery and visual verification.

output_examples.input: Create a new presentation with a title slide.
output_examples.output: The ODP file is created at the requested absolute path, and the deck contains the expected slide count.

output_examples.input: Apply a patch that replaces text and inserts a list.
output_examples.output: The patch result reports success for each operation, and the updated deck is saved to disk.

output_examples.input: Export a deck and snapshot one slide.
output_examples.output: The PDF export is written, and a PNG snapshot is available for visual inspection.

best_practices: Use absolute paths for documents, images, media, and exports.
Read slide inventory before targeting shapes in decks that may change.
Use atomic patch mode when partial edits would be difficult to review.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on one-based slide numbering.
Do not use shape indexes when stable shape names are available.
Do not treat snapshots as exact pixel contracts across environments.

faq.question: Does this skill require LibreOffice?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses LibreOffice UNO and the soffice executable for presentation processing.

faq.question: Which presentation formats can it export?
faq.answer: The documented export formats are PDF and PPTX.

faq.question: How are slides numbered?
faq.answer: Slide indexes are zero-based. The first slide is slide 0.

faq.question: Can it work with relative paths?
faq.answer: No. The skill documentation states that UNO-facing paths must be absolute.

faq.question: What does atomic patch mode do?
faq.answer: Atomic mode stops on the first failed operation and restores the original file bytes.

faq.question: Can it add media and images?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports image and media insertion through absolute file paths.
